retry_reason: Put operations are final and cannot be retried; after releasing a reference the object may be freed. Calling again on the same pointer would be a use-after-free risk if non-NULL, or a no-op if NULL.
contract_summary: This function releases the driver's references to three skb objects (evt, acl, sco) by calling dev_kfree_skb_irq on each, and then sets their pointers to NULL. It is a cleanup wrapper used during error handling or teardown to ensure these skbs are properly freed and not reused. The caller must not use these skb pointers after this function returns. The function holds the rxlock during the operation to ensure thread safety. After calling btusb_free_frags, the driver no longer owns these skbs—they are handed back to the networking stack.
